title: Silver Price Forecast: XAG/USD tests upper Bollinger Band while momentum remains subdued
body: Silver (XAG/USD) trims part of its earlier gains on Thursday as geopolitical headlines from the Middle East continue to stir volatility across financial markets.

title: PHP: Hawkish BSP outlook and off-cycle hike – Standard Chartered
body: Standard Chartered economists Jonathan Koh and Edward Lee argue that the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas (BSP) will stay hawkish despite softer growth in the Philippines.

title: DXY rebounds as Iran hope trade unwinds: Is the safe-haven bid back?
body: The morning belonged to the deal: Asia had ripped overnight, with the Nikkei 225 clearing 62,000 for the first time on a session that gained north of 5%, and US futures came in primed for another leg of the Iran ceasefire trade.

title: EUR/USD retreats from intraday highs as Oil prices rebound on Hormuz tensions
body: EUR/USD eases from intraday highs on Thursday as evolving geopolitical developments surrounding tensions in the Middle East help the US Dollar (USD) and Oil prices recover some of their recent losses.

title: IDR: FX curbs and bond support to steady currency – DBS
body: DBS Group Research’s Radhika Rao highlights Bank Indonesia’s latest steps to manage FX volatility and support the Indonesian Rupiah. The central bank has tightened documentation thresholds for Dollar purchases, continued bond buying and enabled selected dealers to access offshore NDFs.

title: Crude Oil claws back as Hormuz blockade outlasts deal hopes
body: Crude Oil markets staged a sharp intraday reversal on Thursday, with West Texas Intermediate (WTI) futures briefly tagging lows below $90 a barrel before clawing back above $93, while Brent slid toward $95 before recovering to reclaim the $100 handle.

title: Fed’s Daly: We have to work on price stability without overreacting
body: Mary Daly, President of the Federal Reserve (Fed) Bank of San Francisco, said on Bloomberg TV on Thursday that she doesn’t see evidence that longer-run inflation expectations are up. She also claimed that wage rises are consistent with the overall 2% inflation.

title: USD/SGD: Range trading with downside bias – OCBC
body: OCBC strategists Sim Moh Siong and Christopher Wong see USD/SGD consolidating as markets await clarity on a potential US–Iran deal. The pair has slipped slightly on tentative optimism, but Iran has yet to respond and a 48‑hour deadline looms, keeping some caution in place.

title: NY Fed survey: Households see higher inflation next year
body: The New York Federal Reserve (Fed) released its Survey of Consumer Expectations for April on Thursday, which showed that American households expect prices to rise in the short term. For the medium- to long-term horizon, inflation expectations were unchanged.

title: Gold eases from two-week highs as Oil rebounds on Hormuz tensions
body: Gold (XAU/USD) trims some of its earlier gains on Thursday as the US Dollar and Oil prices rebound amid persistent tensions surrounding the Strait of Hormuz. At the time of writing, XAU/USD is trading around $4,712 after touching a two-week high near $4,764 earlier in the day.

title: Dow Jones Industrial Average reverses as Iran deal hopes hit hard reality
body: The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) was off around 0.4% in Thursday's early afternoon trade, sliding back below 50,000 after tagging an intraday high near 50,100.

title: Silver price rallies to three-week highs as Oil slump, softer Dollar lift XAG/USD
body: Silver (XAG/USD) extends its strong rally on Thursday, trading around $80.60 at the time of writing, up 4.19% on the day. XAG/USD is reaching three-week highs, supported by lower Oil prices, declining US Treasury yields and broad weakness in the US Dollar (USD).

title: Gold Price Forecast: XAU/USD struggles to recover its long-term momentum
body: Spot Gold advances for a third consecutive day, hitting an intraday high of $4,765 before retreating some.

title: NOK/SEK: Is the Nordic divergence only getting started?
body: The NOK/SEK pair highlights an increasingly clear divergence between two neighboring economies that are often seen as similar, but are exposed to very different drivers. The current context favors the Norwegian Krone (NOK) over its Swedish counterpart, but for how long?

title: AUD/USD Price Forecast: Bullish near multi-year top amid Iran peace deal hopes, hawkish RBA
body: The AUD/USD pair attracts some buyers for the third consecutive day on Thursday and remains within striking distance of its highest level since June 2022, around the 0.7275-0.7280 region, which it touched the previous day.

title: Gold Price Forecast: XAU/USD confirms a falling wedge breakout on US-Iran peace deal optimism
body: Gold is gathering pace early Thursday, after having hit one-week highs above $4,700 on Tuesday. Markets remain expectant of a potential US-Iran peace deal being reached, supporting the renewed uptrend in Gold.

title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: The 0.7300 hurdle comes next
body: The Aussie Dollar is attempting to break above the ongoing multi-day consolidative phase, with AUD/USD reaching fresh yearly peaks near the 0.7300 barrier. For now, the pair’s positive outlook remains unchanged, propped up by elevated inflation in Oz and the RBA’s cautious stance.

title: XAG/USD Price analysis: Silver recovers its spark, next hurdle at $80
body: Silver edged higher on Wednesday, as optimism put a halt to US Dollar (USD) demand. Market players welcomed headlines indicating that the United States (US) and Iran were closing in on a deal to end the Middle East conflict.

title: The Fed Warsh inherits: Divided and with its independence in doubt
body: The Federal Reserve is the central bank of the world’s most powerful economy, and prides itself on its independence. But is it? Or, better said, will it continue to be under Kevin Warsh?

title: USD/JPY Price Forecast: Seems vulnerable amid suspected JPY intervention, weaker USD
body: The USD/JPY pair attracts heavy intraday selling on Wednesday and dives to the 155.00 psychological mark, or the lowest level since February 24, though it lacks follow-through.

title: All restrictions on Belarusian athletes lifted by Olympic bosses
body: The International Olympic Committee has lifted all restrictions on Belarusian athletes.

title: Three women with alleged links to ISIS set to be charged after arriving in Australia
body: Three women with alleged links to the Islamic State group's former so-called caliphate in Syria are set to face charges, including slavery and terror offences, Australian police have said.

title: 'Unbelievably lucky' no one harmed after megatsunami
body: A tsunami at a popular tourist spot in Alaska was the second highest ever recorded and it was "unbelievably lucky" no one was hurt, researchers have said.

title: EU waters down landmark AI regulation amid industry pressure
body: Overnight, EU lawmakers agreed to water down their AI legislation that was world-leading when it launched in 2024.

title: An extraordinary watershed moment passed below the radar - and helped make Trump's war possible
body: Load up a satellite map of Texas and find your way to a city called Midland, all the way over in the west of the state.
